suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that rerun workers must mark skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false and record deviations whenever they implement before adding regression tests or skip the documented manual-verification step, and explicitly note when isolated temp-repo semantic checks are an acceptable substitute for SSH verification on git command-construction fixes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-30` requires writing tests before implementation and `:80-82` documents manual verification for SSH-related features. The transcript skeleton for worker session `9d34cb54-ca6e-4298-a220-51704b75f247` shows baseline tests, then edits to `packages/git-ops/src/errors.ts`, `src/index.ts`, `src/types.ts`, and `src/live.ts`, followed later by test edits/creation (`packages/git-ops/test/security.test.ts`, `packages/git-ops/test/leading-dash.test.ts`). The handoff `/home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-15T20-36-24-308Z__fix-git-ops-leading-dash__9d34cb54-ca6e-4298-a220-51704b75f247.json:16-26,138-140` records only automated validators and still marks `followedProcedure` true with an empty deviations list.
